codeReview.summary: benchmark-data.json has correct top-level schema and values, but scripts/collect_llama_benchmarks.py is unchanged from its introduction commit and still has all three original bugs. The data fix was a one-time manual file rewrite in bde842a39, not a re-run of the Python script.

issues.description: parse_output() regex searches for literal strings 'prefill' and 'decode' but llama-bench emits 'pp<N>' (pp512, pp2048) for prefill/prompt runs and 'tg<N>' (tg128) for decode/target-gen runs. The regex never matches any line, so prefill and decode remain None. Every entry in the serialized JSON shows null prefill_tokens_per_second and null decode_tokens_per_second already — evidence in commit 9d686f933 diff.

parse_output() captures at most one prefill and one decode value per entire invocation (prefill/decode/peak_vram are module-level None and guarded by 'if X is None'). With per-context 3-run loops (ctx=512/2048/8192), only the first match is ever captured — context-size granularity is lost.

commit 9d686f933 the -o json flag is not actually used or implemented; parse_output() only tries to parse text/markdown output without the flag; the command is `[llama-bench, -m, model, -p, ctx, -n, 128]` – no `-o json` flag appears in the subprocess call

main() still writes the old schema: top-level keys 'model' and 'benchmarks', benchmark entries with 'gpu_name', 'gfx', 'rocm_visible_devices', 'vram_gb', 'model_family'. This is the schema that was FIXED manually in commit bde842a39 directly into validation/benchmark-data.json. Re-running the unmodified script would silently overwrite the corrected JSON with the wrong schema again.

commit 9d686f933 the MAJOR issue: the script was IN FACT NOT EDITED by the fix commit — 'git diff 9d686f933..bde842a39 -- scripts/collect_llama_benchmarks.py' returns identical content. The fix was entirely a data-file content change in validation/benchmark-data.json

sharedStateObservations.observation: The benchmark-data.json schema was silently rewritten in a 'docs' commit (bde842a39) rather than 'benchmark:' or 'fix(' prefix, making it unclear that this was a bug fix rather than a doc-only change. The convention in this repo for actual fixes uses 'fix(' or 'benchmark:' prefixes per the git log.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: bde842a39 message = 'docs: fix benchmark data json schema'; only changed validation/benchmark-data.json, not scripts/

sharedStateObservations.observation: Feature task outcome does not verify that the script actually WORKED — the process has to manually validate that the script was actually fixed and run; requires an execution model

summary: FAIL: benchmark-data.json is currently correct (right schema, right values, right VRAM) thanks to a manual data-file rewrite in commit bde842a39. However scripts/collect_llama_benchmarks.py — the very script supposed to produce this file — is completely unchanged from the broken commit 9d686f933, still has all three original bugs (wrong regex, single-value capture, wrong output schema). The fix commit only touched the JSON data, not the script. All blocking issues remain open in the Python script.
